title: phacelia scopulina
intro: Phacelia scopulina, also known as Scouler's phacelia, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family. It is native to the northwestern United States and southwestern Canada, and is often used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Phacelia scopulina can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for topical application, and is often used to treat minor cuts and scrapes, as well as to soothe skin irritations and promote tissue repair.
